mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 6.0% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in 70% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 6.0%, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE stays above 6.0% for more than 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal income and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and construction demand expansion, which boosts corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive movement of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns, or the interaction term shows a negative coefficient in a multivariate regression over a 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase reflects accelerating factory output and capacity utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying and positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during recovery phases do not precede positive 5-day returns, or the signal reverses direction when capacity utilization exceeds 85%.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising wage income, which supports consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple business cycles, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid inflation acceleration above 0.4% monthly forces expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL rises above 0.4% monthly, or the signal fails to predict negative returns when the Federal Reserve explicitly rules out rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of declining real GDP growth and rising unemployment indicates a synchronized economic contraction, which reduces corporate profit margins and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint deterioration of GDPC1 and UNRATE fails to predict negative 60-day returns, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in industrial production signals weakening manufacturing demand and falling corporate revenues, which triggers risk-off selling and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in 65% of instances where INDPRO falls below the 0.5% monthly threshold, or the signal shows no predictive power in a walk-forward test.
